title: Feature: Optimize operation logic by referencing macOS Finder
author: zbt00123
body:
### Description

## Additional Case 1: Double‑click behavior inconsistency in Columns view

**Folder structure:**
Desktop
    └─ Test_A
            ├─ Layer_1
            │        ├─ 2D
            │        ├─ 3D
            │        ├─ Comp
            │        │        ├─ (Footage)
            │        │        └─ Word_A.txt
            │        └─ Refs
            └─ Word_B.txt


**Observation (Columns view enabled):**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f3926f05-06ee-4ff5-a0f8-5262ccd4d424

-------- macOS --------

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4640994a-520b-4647-bbbb-28afb9e28b08

-------- Files --------

- In **macOS Finder**, as shown in the attached video, after double‑clicking `Word_A.txt` to open it, I can immediately double‑click `Word_B.txt` without any unexpected window jumps. The navigation remains smooth and predictable.

- In **Files**, however, due to the current window‑handling logic, double‑clicking `Word_B.txt` results in the following misbehavior:
  1. The first click of the double‑click selects `Word_B.txt`, which triggers a window navigation (folder content jumps).
  2. Because the window jumps during that first click, the second click of the double‑click is no longer registered on `Word_B.txt` – instead, it lands on the empty background area of the desktop (or an unintended region).

**Impact:**  
This breaks the intuitive double‑click workflow, especially when users need to quickly open multiple files in succession. The erratic navigation forces users to re‑acquire the target file, reducing efficiency and causing confusion.

**Suggested alignment:**  
Adopt the robust focus and navigation behavior seen in macOS Finder, where double‑click actions are reliably processed without intermediate window jumps, ensuring that both clicks of the double‑click are consistently applied to the intended file.

## Additional Case 2: Inconsistent folder creation and paste target logic

**Environment comparison:** macOS Finder vs. Files

**Folder structure (initial):**
- Desktop contains a folder `Test_A`.
- Inside `Test_A`, we will create subfolders for testing.

---

### macOS Finder behavior (expected)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/40bf5bc8-60f8-4678-852e-099bf972b8f6

1. Open macOS Finder. On the desktop, create a folder named `Test_A`, and inside it create a subfolder `AAAA`.
2. Click on the desktop, then create a new folder named `BBB` on the desktop.
3. Go back to the `Test_A` folder, select the `AAAA` folder, and press `Command+C` to copy it.
4. Click on the `BBB` folder (in the view), then press `Command+V`. The `AAAA` folder is correctly pasted **inside** the `BBB` folder.

**Result:** The copy‑paste operation respects the currently selected destination folder.

---

### Files behavior (actual)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4578eb09-dffc-40ca-8d86-ff0eb82518d8

1. Open Files. On the desktop, create a folder named `Test_A`. Click into `Test_A`, then **right‑click** on the right‑side area of the view and choose “Create folder” to create `AAAA`. However, the newly created `AAAA` folder appears on the **desktop** instead of inside `Test_A` (because the right‑click context menu is tied to the desktop background, not the current folder view).
2. Delete the mistakenly created `AAAA` folder. Re‑enter `Test_A` by clicking on it, then click again on the right‑side list area inside `Test_A` to ensure focus is inside the folder. Create `AAAA` again – this time it is correctly placed inside `Test_A`.
3. Click on the desktop area (outside Files) to create a folder named `BBB` on the desktop.
4. Return to `Test_A`, select the `AAAA` folder, and press `Ctrl+C` to copy it.
5. Click on the `BBB` folder (in the Files view) and press `Ctrl+V`. The paste operation places the `AAAA` folder on the **desktop** instead of inside `BBB`.
6. Delete the folder that was pasted on the desktop. Then click on the `BBB` folder, and **click again** on the right‑side list area inside `BBB` to ensure focus is within that folder. Finally, press `Ctrl+V`. Only now does the `AAAA` folder get pasted **inside** `BBB` as intended.

* PS：As shown in the video, even during the first attempt to paste the `AAAA` folder, Files did not properly execute the copy command for that folder (i.e., the copy operation was not successfully triggered).

---

### Analysis of the issue

- In Files, the destination for paste operations is not consistently determined by the currently selected folder in the navigation pane or view. Instead, it often defaults to the background (desktop) unless the user explicitly clicks inside the folder's content area to set focus.
- This forces users to perform extra clicks to re‑establish focus, which is unintuitive and leads to accidental placement of folders on the desktop.

**Impact:**  
Users may misplace copied items, causing confusion and requiring extra cleanup steps. The workflow is less efficient and error‑prone compared to macOS Finder.

**Suggested improvement:**  
Align the paste‑target logic with macOS Finder: when a folder is selected (highlighted) in the view, paste operations should target that folder directly, without requiring an additional click inside its content area. This would eliminate the redundant focus‑setting step and make file management more predictable.

### Steps To Reproduce

As mentioned above.

### Files Version

4.2.0.0

### Windows Version

10.0.26200.8737

### User ID

5be1ab97-a6f8-4ceb-8e51-64d2d86df27a

### Log File

[debug.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29999440/debug.log)

title: Bug: Scrolling can stop working after deleting files, especially in grid view
author: truman-t3
body:
### Description

After deleting files in a folder, scrolling can intermittently stop working in the current folder view. This is especially noticeable when using the grid/icon layout.

The rest of the application remains usable, but the folder view no longer responds to scrolling. Navigating away from the folder and returning may restore scrolling.

This appears related to, but has a different trigger from, #17873 (drag & drop breaks scrolling) and may also be related to #14964 (scrolling freezes in icon/grid view).

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Open a folder containing multiple files.
2. Switch to a grid/icon layout.
3. Delete one or more files.
4. Try to scroll within the same folder using the mouse wheel, scrollbar, or keyboard.

### Files Version

4.1.1.0

### Windows Version

Windows 11 Home China, version 26200.8655

### User ID

_No response_

### Log File

[debug.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29960910/debug.log)

title: Bug: Sometimes the QuickLook window fails to activate.
author: zbt00123
body:
### Description

**Issue Description**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1e30fa70-09c7-4b6b-ad41-b6533ef6ece7

> **Additional Notes (from the demo video):**
> - In the video, the left window is **Files** and the right window is **Explorer** .  
> - The label "空格" shown in the lower‑lift corner of the video refers to the **Space** key.  
> - In **Explorer**, QuickLook works perfectly fine; this issue only occurs in **Files**.

I have installed the [QuickLook](https://github.com/QL-Win/QuickLook/) on my PC, which allows Windows users to quickly preview selected files or folders by pressing the **Space** key, just like macOS users do.

However, I encountered a bug in **Files**:

1. I select `Test A.jpg` and press the **Space** key — the QuickLook preview window opens normally.  
2. I press the **Space** key again to close the preview window.  
3. Then, if I click on `Test A.jpg` again (without deselecting it first) and press the **Space** key, the preview window **does not** launch.  
4. The only way to restore the preview functionality via the **Space** key is to:
   - Click on an empty area, or select another file to deselect `Test A.jpg`, and then re‑select `Test A.jpg`.
5. After doing that, pressing the **Space** key works again as expected.

### Steps To Reproduce

**Steps to reproduce the bug in Files:**

1. Open the **"BUG"** folder using **Files**.
2. Select [`Test A.jpg`](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/97be840f-8e97-46d7-8272-45a4cd40e707) and press the **Space** key.
3. After the QuickLook preview window opens, press the **Space** key again to close it.
4. Directly click on the already‑selected `Test A.jpg` (without deselecting it) and press the **Space** key — the QuickLook window **does not** activate.
5. Click on an empty area, or select another file ([Test B.png](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d263fdd2-d9eb-4001-b24a-644d6dc49218)) /folder to deselect `Test A.jpg`.
6. Click on `Test A.jpg` again to re‑select it, then press the **Space** key — the QuickLook window activates successfully.

**Expected behavior (observed in File Explorer):**  
In **File Explorer**, no matter what operations are performed, pressing the **Space** key at any time will successfully open the QuickLook window. This issue occurs **only** in **Files**.

### Files Version

4.2.0.0

### Windows Version

10.0.26200.8737

### User ID

5be1ab97-a6f8-4ceb-8e51-64d2d86df27a

### Log File

[debug.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29809753/debug.log)

title: Bug: After changing the folder name, renaming a subfolder will cause an error.
author: zbt00123
body:
### Description

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/76478f65-397a-44de-b401-93462f47daa9

1. This file is being used by another program.
2. Item not found
3. Has this folder been deleted?

### Steps To Reproduce

1. In the "Test" folder, select the "CCC" folder and rename it to "Test_C".
2. After typing "Test_C", directly click on the "123" folder inside the "CCC" subfolder. This triggers **BUG-1**:
3. You must now click on a blank area of the window, or any folder outside the "CCC" folder, to clear this bug state.
4. Now rename the "CCC" folder again, and it can be successfully renamed to "Test_C".
5. Now you can directly select the "123" folder without triggering **BUG-1**.
6. After selecting the "123" folder, click the rename button and change the name to "ABC". This triggers **BUG-2**.
7. Click on a blank area, then select the "123" folder again. This triggers **BUG-3**.
8. Select a different folder at a higher level to clear the bug state. Then go back to the "123" folder, and you can rename it freely.

### Files Version

4.2.0.0

### Windows Version

10.0.26200.8737

### User ID

5be1ab97-a6f8-4ceb-8e51-64d2d86df27a

### Log File

[debug.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29804596/debug.log)

title: Bug: Ứng dụng File load các file rất chậm.
author: minhquan99990
body:
### Description

Như tiêu đề trên, ứng dụng File load các file rất chậm. Tôi biết File là ứng dụng ngoài windows nhưng tôi còn thích sử dụng File hơn cả Windows Explorer nhưng có 1 nhược điểm làm tôi rất khó chịu đó là ứng dụng File load các file rất chậm. Mong nhà phát triển đọc được đóng góp này sớm nhất và fix lỗi này sớm nhất có thể.

### Steps To Reproduce
- Steps To Reproduce chắc chắn không có rồi vì thứ này xuất hiện ở khắp nơi trên ứng dụng File. Thay vào đó, tôi sẽ đề xuất cho tác giả.
- Để giảm load file chậm, tôi đề xuất như sau:
1. Load File đến đâu hiện file đến đó: Có nghĩa là trong khi File đang load thì hãy hiện luôn các file đã load. Điều này có thể che đi sự chậm chạp của File.
2. Hãy tối ưu File như Apple: Chắc Developer cũng biết là Apple luôn tối ưu mọi thứ rất tốt. Bạn hãy tối ưu File lại để góp phần lớn nhất giảm load file chậm cho File

### Files Version

4.2.0.0

### Windows Version

10.0.26200.8737

### User ID

40ed4ad3-7859-49e2-88ea-8fc58a598060

### Log File

[debug.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29781809/debug.log)

title: Bug: 某些情况下无法打开回收站
author: zbt00123
body: ### Description 如图所示，这是我的设置： ## 如视频所示，这是BUG的复现情况： https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fd036335-34c2-4548-a6ae-9edda98e33ff ## 目前，Files进程在后台运行中，也仍然会面临窗口关闭后，再次打开Files或者文件夹，启动Files的速度有些慢。我的CPU型号是13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900KS。 ### Steps To Reproduce 1. 双击打开了桌面上的“AE Test”文件夹。 2. 按Ctrl+W关闭了窗口。 3. 双击打开“回收站”，回收站窗口没有启动，但任务管理器中，Files进程有波动。 4. 右键单击“回收站”，选择“打开”，回收站窗口没有启动，但任务管理器中，Files进程有波动。 5. 再次右键单击“回收站”，选择“打开”，回收站终于启动。 注：这个BUG出现的概率很高，包括启动Files后，可能在“主页”中会重复显示多个D盘，或者同时重复显示多个C盘和D盘。 ### Files Version 4.2.0.0 ### Windows Version 10.0.26200.8737 ### User ID 5be1ab97-a6f8-4ceb-8e51-64d2d86df27a ### Log File [debug.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29753193/debug.log)

title: Bug: Performance isn't great
author: ShadowFireWolf
body:
### Description

I have an HD drive that has a folder of videos within subfolders. When I went to this folder, it took a few moments for the app to display a thumbnail for each folder. I opened each folder (33 in total) and allowed the app to display the thumbnails. When I went up a level, it took the app a few seconds to display the thumbnails. When I opened a subfolder, the same delay occurred; this doesn't happen with the windows 11 file explorer.



### Steps To Reproduce

1. Open the app (installed on an SSD)
2. Navigate to the HD (D drive)
3. Open the Video folder and subfolders.
4. Notice that the thumbnails aren't automatically displayed and take a moment.
